executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: Architecture-owned verifier/runtime topology is coherent; the only live red remains marketing because primary-repo adoption is still flat and marketing independent verification is fail.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Do not recertify the full stack until the marketing loop produces a fresh independent pass backed by measurable Codeberg movement.

audit_metadata.scheduler_inspection_method: direct openclaw cron list --json plus jobs.json history split and live artifact cross-checks

owns: The live Gateway-owned verifier-supervisor job is the scheduler authority for this docs loop; direct user crontab entries must remain absent.

Runtime health monitor with bounded repair authority only.
Must not inspect content-loop outcome artifacts or trigger cross-owner remediation except explicit timeout reruns on the affected job.
Any material change to this monitor requires fresh independent verification.
Generic blocked-channel ownership now lives under explicitly non-Reddit names to avoid marketing ownership leakage.
This loop was collapsed to one owner schedule after the old deep-review/follow-up split was found to share one runtime path and one result artifact.
Whole-system audit loop that verifies architecture health across owner boundaries.
When it repairs another repair/recovery loop, it must also arrange fresh independent verification before claiming that path healthy.
Owns pushing workspace research findings into the git repo with tests and sync proof.
Operational support loop; not a self-certifying quality authority.
Gateway-owned Codeberg-to-GitHub mirror sync. Codeberg remains source of truth.
Operational replication loop; ownership must stay explicit even though it is not a full verifier contract.

findings.title: Marketing remains the only live red owner loop
findings.mechanism: marketing independent verification is still fail and the latest workflow audit still shows flat Codeberg and GitHub adoption deltas across the recent window.
findings.root_cause: Outcome evidence is still missing on the Codeberg-primary conversion lane.
findings.recommended_fix: Let the marketing owner loop produce fresh measurable outcome evidence, then rerun marketing independent verification before architecture calls the whole stack green.

findings.title: Architecture audit metadata now matches the live Gateway topology
findings.mechanism: Live runtime currently shows 20 jobs, 20 enabled, 0 disabled, and the refreshed audit matches that state.
findings.root_cause: Direct live inspection and the architecture artifact are aligned in this run.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep direct live-topology checks on every watchdog run; do not infer live-disabled jobs from persisted history.

findings.title: Architecture verifier path is ready for fresh qualified signoff once the refreshed audit is rechecked
findings.mechanism: checker is green and the remaining architecture verifier status is qualified-pass because the only live blocker is external marketing evidence, not an architecture-owned fault.
findings.root_cause: The audit artifact lagged the current live cron topology.
findings.recommended_fix: Rerun independent verification and the architecture verifier immediately after refreshing the audit artifacts.

findings.title: Docs remains independently green and stable
findings.mechanism: Docs verifier is pass and does not contribute a live architecture fault.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Wait for a marketing lane that can move primary-repo adoption and rerun marketing independent verification on the resulting evidence
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Marketing is still the only red owner domain after the architecture-side runtime checks were refreshed.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Either clears the remaining blocker or sharpens the next marketing repair target.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Keep architecture live-topology verification tied to openclaw cron list --json on every watchdog run
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: This was the concrete blocker in the current architecture verifier failure.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Prevents persisted-history drift from being misreported as a live architecture fault.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Only call the whole stack green after both architecture and marketing independent verification artifacts are fresh
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Current architecture health is qualified by an external marketing blocker, not a local verifier gap.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Keeps certification honest across owner boundaries.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Refreshed the architecture audit against the current 20-job live Gateway topology and latest verifier/health-monitor state.
Reran independent verification against the refreshed live runtime evidence.
Reran the architecture verifier; architecture-owned verification stays green with a qualified pass because marketing remains externally red.

independent_verification.summary: Independent verification confirms the repaired architecture verifier now fails closed on stale signoff, the live loop topology/ownership checks remain green, and shared market-intelligence reuse stays machine-verifiable.

what_still_needs_independent_verificatio: Fresh marketing independent pass backed by measurable primary-repo movement.

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Marketing remains red on Codeberg-primary outcome evidence
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: Architecture-owned runtime checks are coherent, but Codeberg adoption is still flat and marketing independent verification still fails closed.

notes: Architecture green here means architecture-owned localization is healthy; it does not mean marketing outcome evidence is green.
Persisted disabled jobs remain history only; live disabled jobs are zero.
Independent live verification in this audit snapshot saw 20 total jobs, 20 enabled, 0 disabled, 0 running, and 0 live last-error residues.
The remaining blocker is marketing outcome evidence, not an architecture self-certification fault.
